title: Warm Flaky Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Moroccan dish featuring tender lamb, sweet and sticky Dutch stroop, and a blend of Mediterranean spices, all slow-cooked to perfection in a tandoor-clay-pot.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor-clay-pot to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, Dutch stroop, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Heat the tandoor-clay-pot over medium heat and brown the lamb pieces on all sides.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
